title: Heat exchanger system
abstract: Disclosed is a heat exchanger system comprising, a first heat exchanger comprising at least one condenser unit and at least one first evaporator unit, the at least one condenser unit arranged adjacent to the at least one first evaporator unit; a second heat exchanger comprising a second evaporator unit; a central portion positioned adjacent to at least one of the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger; the central portion configured to house a ventilation device; a secondary coolant container operable to direct secondary coolant to the at least one condenser unit to cool the condenser unit; a compressor unit adapted to direct a primary coolant to the at least one condenser unit and to receive the primary coolant from the first and second evaporator units, wherein the compressor unit is suited for a low temperature and low-pressure application. Further disclosed are different configurations of heat exchanger devices for use in a heat exchanger system.
claims:
Claims 1. A heat exchanger system comprising a first heat exchanger comprising at least one condenser unit and at least one first evaporator unit, the at least one condenser unit arranged adjacent to the at least one first evaporator unit; a second heat exchanger comprising a second evaporator unit; a central portion positioned adjacent to at least one of the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger; the central portion configured to house a ventilation device; a secondary coolant container operable to direct secondary coolant to the at least one condenser unit to cool the condenser unit; a compressor unit adapted to direct a primary coolant to the at least one condenser unit and to receive the primary coolant from the first and second evaporator units; wherein the compressor unit is suited for a low temperature and low-pressure application. 2. The heat exchanger system of claim 1, wherein the secondary coolant container is in the form of a water tank, the water tank operable to direct water to the at least one condenser unit via a sprinkler mechanism, the sprinkler mechanism positioned in the proximity of the at least one condenser unit; and the heat exchanger system further comprises a first channel to receive water from the at least one first evaporator unit, wherein the first channel directs water back to the water tank. 3. The heat exchanger system of claims 1 or 2, wherein the water tank comprises a second channel connected externally for refill of the water tank by an external source. 4. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the at least one condenser unit comprises a first heat exchanger device and a second heat exchanger device arranged adjoining the first heat exchanger device, and the second heat exchanger device is arranged adjoining the at least one first evaporator unit. 5. The heat exchanger system of claim 4, wherein the first heat exchanger device comprises: a first conduit having a water inlet pipe adapted to connect to the water tank; a second conduit contained within the first conduit or surrounded by the first conduit, the second conduit having a refrigerant inlet adapted to be connected to the compressor unit to receive primary coolant in the form of a refrigerant, wherein the second conduit comprises a first spiral portion and a second spiral portion, the first spiral portion and the second spiral portion are arranged to maximize the surface area of the respective spiral portions exposed to the first conduit. 6. The heat exchanger system of claim 5, wherein the second spiral portion of the first heat exchanger device is contained or surrounded by the first spiral portion of the first heat exchanger device. 7. The heat exchanger system of claim 5 or 6, wherein the water inlet pipe and the refrigerant inlet are positioned on a first side of the first heat exchanger device, and wherein there is a water outlet pipe and a refrigerant outlet positioned on a second side of the first heat exchanger device, wherein the second side is opposite the first side. 8. The heat exchanger system of claim 4, wherein the second heat exchanger device comprises: a first conduit having a water inlet pipe adapted to connect to the water tank; a second conduit contained within the first conduit or surrounded by the first conduit, the second conduit having a refrigerant inlet adapted to be connected to the at least one first evaporator unit to receive refrigerant, wherein the second conduit comprises a first spiral portion and a second spiral portion, the first spiral portion and the second spiral portion are arranged to maximize the surface area of the respective spiral portions exposed to the first conduit. 9. The heat exchanger system of claim 8, wherein the first and second spiral portions of the second heat exchanger device are arranged such that a body portion of the first and second spiral portions overlaps each other. 10. The heat exchanger system of claims 8 or 9, wherein the water inlet pipe, a first part of the refrigerant inlet, and a first part of a refrigerant outlet are positioned on a first side of the second heat exchanger device, and wherein a water outlet pipe, a second part of the refrigerant inlet, and a second part of the refrigerant outlet are positioned on a second side of the second heat exchanger device, wherein the second side is opposite to the first side. 11. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 4 to 10, wherein the at least one first evaporator unit is arranged to receive cooled refrigerant from the first heat exchanger device for absorbing heat from an ambient environment, and wherein the second heat exchanger device is arranged to receive refrigerant from the at least one first evaporator unit and water from the water tank for cooling the water. 12. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 8 to 11, wherein the water outlet pipe of the first heat exchanger device is adapted to be connected to the water inlet pipe of the second heat exchanger, such that the first and second heat exchanger devices are linked. 13. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 4 to 12, wherein cooled refrigerant is sent from the first heat exchanger device to the at least one first evaporator unit and/or the second evaporator unit via a flow control device operable to control the flow of the cooled refrigerant to the at least one first evaporator unit and/or the second evaporator unit. 14. The heat exchanger system of claim 13, wherein the refrigerant outlet positioned on the second side of the first heat exchanger device is adapted to connect to a first end of the flow control device, wherein a second end of the flow control device is adapted to connect to the at least one first evaporator unit, such that the cooled refrigerant is sent from the first heat exchanger device to the at least one first evaporator unit, and wherein the first end is opposite to the second end. 15. The heat exchanger system of claims 13 or 14, wherein the flow control device comprises a plurality of capillary tubes. 16. The heat exchanger system of claim 15, wherein the flow control device comprises a first capillary tube and a second capillary tube arranged adjacent to the first capillary tube. 17. The heat exchanger system of claim 16, wherein the first capillary tube is spaced apart from the second capillary tube by a distance of 0.5 mm. 18. The heat exchanger system of claims 16 or 17, wherein a portion of the first and second capillary tubes are arranged to form a helical coil, and wherein the helical coil is enclosed in a casing. 19. The heat exchanger system of claim 18, wherein the casing further comprises a first opening positioned on a first side, and a second opening positioned on a second side, the first and second openings adapted to surround the first and second capillary tubes, and wherein either the first or second opening comprises an angled opening, such that the first and second capillary tubes abuts the opening at an angle, and wherein the first side is opposite to the second side. 20. The heat exchanger system of claim 19, wherein the casing comprises a fluid inlet positioned on the first side, the fluid inlet adapted to receive a fluid, a fluid outlet positioned on the second side, the fluid outlet adapted to release the fluid from the casing. 21. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 5 to 20, wherein at least one of the first and second conduits are formed of or formed from copper. 22. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 7 to 21, wherein at least one of the water inlet pipe and the water outlet pipe are a 12.7 mm diameter pipe. 23. The heat exchanger system of claim 22, wherein at least one of the refrigerant inlet and the refrigerant outlet are a 6.35 mm diameter pipe. 24. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 22 to 23, wherein the first conduit has an inner diameter of 102 mm. 25. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 21 to 24, wherein the first conduit has a length of about 350 mm. 26. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 4 to 18, wherein the ratio of surface area between the first heat exchanger device and the second heat exchanger device is in the range from 1:3 to 1:1. 27. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 1 to 26, wherein the compressor unit utilizes an HFC refrigerant, preferably a R134 refrigerant. 28. The heat exchanger system of any one of claim 1 to 27, wherein the central portion further houses an exhaust unit. 29. The heat exchanger system of any one of claim 1 to 28, wherein the heat exchanger system comprises a housing to contain the compressor unit, the water tank, the central portion, the first heat exchanger, so as to form an integrated portable unit. 30. The heat exchanger system of claim 29, wherein the heat absorbed by the water in the first heat exchanger device is partially or completely removed in the second heat exchanger device such that the heat exchanger system is without an exhaust outlet/hose. 31. The heat exchanger system of any one of claims 1 to 30, further comprising a timer arranged in communication with the compressor unit, the timer arranged to toggle the compressor unit between an on state and an off state. 32. The heat exchanger system of claim 31, wherein the timer is set to toggle the on state and the off state based on a predetermined timing profile. 33. The heat exchanger system of claim 32, wherein the predetermining timing profile comprises a ratio of on state to off state in the range of 1.5:1 to 9:1. 34. A method of manufacturing a heat exchanger system comprising the steps of (a.) providing a first heat exchanger, the first heat exchanger comprising at least one condenser unit and at least one first evaporator unit, the at least one condenser unit arranged adjacent to the at least one first evaporator unit; (b.) providing a second heat exchanger comprising a second evaporator unit; (c.) providing a central portion positioned adjacent to at least one of the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger; the central portion configured to house a ventilation device; (d.) providing a compressor unit adapted to direct a primary coolant to the at least one condenser unit and to receive the primary coolant from the first and second evaporator units; (e.) providing a secondary coolant container operable to direct secondary coolant to the at least one condenser unit to cool the condenser unit; wherein the compressor unit is suited for a low temperature and low-pressure application. 35. A heat exchanger device comprising a first conduit having a first fluid inlet adapted to connect to a first fluid source; a second conduit contained within the first conduit or surrounded by the first conduit, the second conduit having a second fluid inlet adapted to be connected to a second fluid source; wherein the second conduit comprises a first spiral portion and a second spiral portion, the first spiral portion and the second spiral portion are arranged to maximize the surface area of the respective spiral portions exposed to the first conduit. 36. The heat exchanger device of claim 35, wherein the second spiral portion is contained within the first spiral portion or surrounded by the first spiral portion. 37. The heat exchanger device of claim 35, wherein the second spiral portion overlaps with a portion of the first spiral portion. 38. The heat exchanger device of claims 35 or 36, wherein the first fluid inlet and the second fluid inlet are positioned on a first side of the heat exchanger device, and wherein there is a first fluid outlet and second fluid outlet positioned on a second side of the heat exchanger device, wherein the second side is opposite the first side. 39. The heat exchanger device of claim 35 or 37, wherein the first fluid inlet, a first part of the second fluid inlet, and a first part of a second fluid outlet are positioned on a first side of the heat exchanger device, and wherein a first fluid outlet, a second part of the second fluid inlet, and a second part of the second fluid outlet are positioned on a second side of the heat exchanger device, wherein the second side is opposite the first side. 40. The heat exchanger device of any one of claims 30 to 39, wherein at least one of the first and second conduits may be formed of or formed from copper. 41. The heat exchanger device of any one of claims 35 to 40, wherein at least one of the first fluid inlet and first fluid outlet may be a 12.7 mm diameter pipe. 42. The heat exchanger device of claim 41, wherein at least one of the second fluid inlet and the second fluid outlet may be a 6.35 mm diameter pipe. 43. The heat exchanger device of claim 41 or 42, wherein the first conduit has an inner diameter of 102 mm. 44. The heat exchanger device of any one of claims 40 to 43, wherein the first conduit has a length of about 350 mm. 45. A method of manufacturing a heat exchanger device comprising the steps of (a.) providing a first conduit, the first conduit having a first fluid inlet adapted to connect to a first fluid source; 

 (b.) providing a second conduit, the second conduit contained within the first conduit or surrounded by the first conduit, the second conduit having a second fluid inlet adapted to be connected to a second fluid source; wherein the second conduit comprises a first spiral portion and a second spiral portion, the first spiral portion and the second spiral portion are arranged to maximize the surface area of the respective spiral portions exposed to the first conduit.
